items.title: UMAP workflow from high-D data to 2D plot
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  raw["High-D matrix X"] --> scale["Optional scaling"]
  scale --> knn["Build k-NN graph n_neighbors"]
  knn --> fuzzy["Fuzzy topological representation"]
  fuzzy --> optim["Optimize 2D layout min_dist"]
  optim --> plot["Embedding scatter plot"]
items.caption: UMAP connects local neighborhoods in high dimensions, then optimizes low-dimensional coordinates to match that fuzzy graph.

items.title: n_neighbors controls local vs global emphasis
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  small["Small n_neighbors"] --> local["Fine local clusters"]
  large["Large n_neighbors"] --> global["Broader global layout"]
  local --> trade["Same data can look different"]
  global --> trade
items.caption: Smaller neighborhoods highlight fine structure; larger neighborhoods stitch distant regions into one coherent map.

items.title: Global vs local structure in embeddings
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  high["High-D distances"] --> localGoal["Preserve nearby pairs"]
  high --> globalGoal["Preserve far-region layout"]
  localGoal --> embed["2D embedding trade-off"]
  globalGoal --> embed
items.caption: Every non-linear embedder balances keeping neighbors close with keeping the big-picture arrangement meaningful—UMAP exposes that trade-off via n_neighbors and min_dist.
